fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly allows the write instead of denying it.

spec.expr: len(recent__1) < limit or len(recent__1) > limit or (len(recent__1) == limit)

constraints.intent: When len(recent) equals limit the branch should be taken (denied), so the condition must use >= not >.
Verify that when recent count equals limit, the subsequent guard correctly denies the request (i.e., >= should be used).
Confirm that the comparison at the branch uses >= so that equality with limit triggers denial.
In the else branch (allowed path), recent count must be strictly less than limit; equality should have been caught by the if-branch.
